title: Count Square Submatrices of Ones
hints: Consider how the largest square ending at a cell depends on its neighbors.
Use dynamic programming where each cell stores the side length of the largest square ending there.

A cell can anchor a square only as large as the smallest square its three already-computed neighbors (up, left, and the diagonal) can support — one big neighbor is wasted if another is small. Notice too that this side length is exactly how many new squares end at that cell, so accumulating those side lengths gives the total.
